summary: The current development landscape across key counties including Cleveland, Mecklenburg, Cabarrus, Gaston, and Union shows robust builder activity and significant land watchlist signals, particularly in Cleveland County with multiple large parcels exhibiting high watch scores indicative of potential development or infrastructure investment. Mecklenburg County presents the strongest locate pattern cluster along Westinghouse Boulevard, overlapping 14 approved residential projects and several parcels with high watch scores, pointing to imminent growth and telecom infrastructure opportunities. Union County evidences a flurry of residential and economic development proceedings, while Cabarrus reveals emerging telecom expansion signals near Concord Mills Boulevard, suggesting fiber network growth. Builder activity remains concentrated in Gaston, Mecklenburg, and Union with active and upcoming single-family home communities, supporting continued residential demand. GIS parcel intelligence and locate correlations highlight Cleveland and Mecklenburg as priority zones for strategic land acquisition and infrastructure deployment, while Union and Cabarrus offer moderate opportunities to leverage telecom and utility construction growth.
key_findings: Cleveland County contains multiple large land parcels (317-546 acres) with highest watch scores (9), predominantly owned by LLCs, signaling major development potential.
Mecklenburg County’s Westinghouse Boulevard cluster shows very high confidence in development/infrastructure activity, with 14 nearby approved projects and 4 high-watch parcels.
Union County has ongoing residential zoning and economic development cases with high priority scores, indicating active land use changes and growth momentum.
Cabarrus County’s locate patterns suggest telecom fiber/utility work near Concord Mills Boulevard with moderate opportunity and presence of high-watch parcels.
Builder activity is prolific in Gaston, Mecklenburg, and Union counties, featuring notable communities from D.R. Horton and Eastwood Homes targeting single-family housing markets.
top_opportunities: Explore acquisition or partnerships on high watch-score parcels in Cleveland County for large-scale development or infrastructure projects.
Engage in Mecklenburg’s Westinghouse Boulevard corridor for telecom fiber deployment leveraging multiple approved residential developments and concentrated land signals.
Capitalize on Union County’s active residential zoning and economic incentive initiatives to support builder pipelines and related utility infrastructure.
Monitor and support telecom expansion signals in Cabarrus to align fiber construction with commercial growth trends around Concord Mills Boulevard.
Collaborate with major homebuilders like D.R. Horton and Eastwood Homes in Gaston and Mecklenburg counties to integrate telecom networks ahead of residential delivery.
telecom_insights: Mecklenburg’s Westinghouse Boulevard presents a compelling telecom construction opportunity with a very high locate pattern confidence and dense project ecosystem.
Directional bore and utility work detected near Concord Mills Boulevard in Cabarrus likely indicate fiber network extension amid commercial expansion.
No direct telecom projects found in Union County, but utility construction signals suggest future infrastructure buildout in active development corridors.
High-watch parcels near telecom locate signals increase the potential for coordinated utility and network deployments in Mecklenburg and Cabarrus counties.
The concentrated telecom activity in Mecklenburg supports prioritizing fiber readiness in residential communities to meet emerging demand.
counties_to_watch: Cleveland—due to high-value land parcels with strong watch scores and LLC holdings indicating imminent development.
Mecklenburg—for its dense residential projects cluster and overlapping telecom locate signals creating a strategic infrastructure hub.
Union—highlighted by numerous active land use cases and economic development incentives signaling growth and builder targeting.
Cabarrus—for emerging fiber utility expansion activity near major commercial corridors with high-watch land parcels.
Gaston—due to ongoing builder community sales and potential connections to broader residential growth trends.
recommended_actions: Initiate targeted land acquisition assessments and due diligence in Cleveland County’s top watch-score parcels to position for large-scale projects.
Engage telecom partners to accelerate fiber infrastructure deployment along Westinghouse Boulevard in Mecklenburg County in alignment with residential developments.
Coordinate with Union County planning and economic development offices to support utility infrastructure aligned with new residential and industrial projects.
Monitor and map telecom locate activity in Cabarrus to anticipate and respond to fiber expansion needs before competitors establish networks.
Strengthen builder relationships in Gaston and Mecklenburg counties to offer integrated land development and telecom solutions, enhancing competitive advantage.
